title: Shorea balanocarpoides
intro: Shorea balanocarpoides, also known as Belangkas, is a tropical tree species belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The bark of Shorea balanocarpoides has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Shorea balanocarpoides include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Shorea balanocarpoides include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, often used to treat skin conditions and fever.
taxonomy: Shorea balanocarpoides is classified within the family Dipterocarpaceae, a group of tropical tree species. This family is further divided into several genera, with Shorea being one of the most diverse and widespread. The species Shorea balanocarpoides is also classified as a member of the genus Shorea, which contains around 200 species of trees. These trees are commonly found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and are known for their valuable timber. The classification of Shorea balanocarpoides reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native habitat.
uses: Shorea balanocarpoides is reported to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian countries to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
